summary: reconcileStreetsAction updates/inserts route_street positions one row at a time against the new non-deferrable UNIQUE (route_id, position) while old rows still occupy target positions (removals deleted only after updates), so nearly any non-append edit raises 23505, retried once by withSlugRetry then surfaced as 500.
failure_scenario: Save [A@0, B@1] then PUT with [B, A] (reverse), [A, NEW, B] (insert), or [A, C] (removal): first write to an occupied position hits route_street_route_id_position_key immediately (verified non-deferrable via temp-table repro); transaction rolls back, retry fails identically, endpoint 500s — the PR's own spec test at RouteBuilderControllerSpec.scala:217 exercises exactly this.

summary: Mission case class gained a 17th field (userRouteId) but this pre-existing call site was not updated; branch test sources no longer compile.
failure_scenario: sbt test fails at Test/compileIncremental ('Unspecified value parameter userRouteId', reproduced in the web container) before any test runs, so the whole DB-backed suite including the new RouteBuilderControllerSpec cannot execute.

summary: saveRoute validates name/description but, unlike updateRoute, never rejects an empty streets array; a saved 0-street route later crashes Explore (getRouteDistance 0 → mission None → .map(_.get) throws).
failure_scenario: POST /saveRoute with {"region_id": R, "streets": [], "name": "x"} returns 200; anyone opening /explore?routeId=<id> (e.g. via /r/<slug>) hits NoSuchElementException at ExploreService.scala:250 → 500; route also invisible in listings (inner join on route_street).

summary: #reconstructPath entry-node-within-15m-of-coords[0] test misclassifies streets shorter than ~15-25 m traversed from the far end as entered-at-first, producing flip=false for a segment actually walked in reverse.
failure_scenario: 12 m connector entered at last coordinate: distanceM(entryNode, coords[0]) ≈ 12 < 15 → flip=false; arrows render wrong direction and route_street.reverse is wrong, so Explore starts the user at the wrong end.

summary: #submit has no in-flight guard and is bound to both the confirm click and the name input's Enter keydown, so double-click (or Enter+click while pending) fires POST /saveRoute multiple times, creating duplicate routes.
failure_scenario: Double-click 'Save route': two POSTs race, server saves two identical routes (second gets slug '<name>-2'), onSaved runs twice, saved-routes list/dashboard shows an unintended duplicate (guest localStorage gets both too).

summary: The Downs re-add of UNIQUE (route_id, street_edge_id) fails as soon as any route legitimately contains the same street twice (the out-and-back case this evolution explicitly enables), aborting the Downs mid-sequence.
failure_scenario: After deploy, a user builds an out-and-back route (street S at two positions); a later rollback runs 344's Downs: mission.user_route_id and route_slug_alias already dropped, then ADD CONSTRAINT route_street_route_id_street_edge_id_key errors on the duplicate rows — Play halts with the DB half-reverted.
